pi_items.source: Filing rule specified in task.json description: 'Alan Turing's papers go to BOX-TURING'
Filing rule specified in task.json description: 'Claude Shannon's papers go to BOX-SHANNON'
Filing rule specified in task.json description: 'Albert Einstein's papers go to BOX-RELATIVITY'
seed_library.py CLASSIC_PAPERS: 'On Computable Numbers...' with authors [('Alan', 'Turing')]. Author last name matches.
Paper title in seed_library.py: 'On Computable Numbers, with an Application to the Entscheidungsproblem' contains 'Computable Numbers'
seed_library.py CLASSIC_PAPERS: 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' with authors [('Alan', 'Turing')]. Author last name matches.
Paper title in seed_library.py: 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' contains 'Computing Machinery'
seed_library.py CLASSIC_PAPERS: 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' with authors [('Claude E.', 'Shannon')]. Author last name matches.

Paper title in seed_library.py: 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' contains 'A Mathematical Theory'. Confirmed as real 1948 Bell System Technical Journal publication by Claude Shannon via web search.
seed_library.py CLASSIC_PAPERS: 'The Mathematical Theory of Communication' with authors [('Claude E.', 'Shannon'), ('Warren', 'Weaver')]. First author last name is Shannon per filing rule.

Paper title in seed_library.py: 'The Mathematical Theory of Communication' contains 'The Mathematical Theory'. Confirmed as real 1949 University of Illinois Press book by Shannon and Weaver via web search.
seed_library.py CLASSIC_PAPERS: 'On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies' with authors [('Albert', 'Einstein')]. Author last name matches.
Paper title in seed_library.py: 'On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies' contains 'Electrodynamics'. Confirmed as real 1905 Annalen der Physik publication by Albert Einstein via web search.

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data: Papers are historical references, but the data is synthesized and inserted into Zotero database by seed_library.py in 'classic' mode during task setup
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh calls 'python3 /workspace/scripts/seed_library.py --mode classic' which inserts paper metadata into /home/ga/Zotero/zotero.sqlite database

privileged_info_summary: All five target papers are real, historically significant academic works: (1) Einstein's 1905 'On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies' published in Annalen der Physik; (2-3) Turing's two papers 'On Computable Numbers' (1936) and 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' (1950) published in Proceedings of the London Mathematical Society and Mind respectively; (4-5) Shannon's 1948 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' and Shannon-Weaver's 1949 book 'The Mathematical Theory of Communication'. The task data is synthetic—created via seed_library.py which programmatically inserts these paper records into the Zotero SQLite database with correct titles and author information. The metadata correctly specifies which papers exist (title fragments match actual titles) and correctly maps first authors to filing boxes (Turing→BOX-TURING, Shannon→BOX-SHANNON, Einstein→BOX-RELATIVITY).
